title: cuRoboV2: Dynamics-Aware Motion Generation with Depth-Fused Distance Fields for High-DoF Robots
title_zh: cuRoboV2：面向高自由度机器人的深度融合距离场动力学感知运动生成
abstract: Effective robot autonomy requires motion generation that is safe, feasible, and reactive. Current methods are fragmented: fast planners output physically unexecutable trajectories, reactive controllers struggle with high-fidelity perception, and existing solvers fail on high-DoF systems. We present cuRoboV2, a unified framework with three key innovations: (1) B-spline trajectory optimization that enforces smoothness and torque limits; (2) a GPU-native TSDF/ESDF perception pipeline that generates dense signed distance fields covering the full workspace, unlike existing methods that only provide distances within sparsely allocated blocks, up to 10x faster and in 8x less memory than the state-of-the-art at manipulation scale, with up to 99% collision recall; and (3) scalable GPU-native whole-body computation, namely topology-aware kinematics, differentiable inverse dynamics, and map-reduce self-collision, that achieves up to 61x speedup while also extending to high-DoF humanoids (where previous GPU implementations fail). On benchmarks, cuRoboV2 achieves 99.7% success under 3kg payload (where baselines achieve only 72--77%), 99.6% collision-free IK on a 48-DoF humanoid (where prior methods fail entirely), and 89.5% retargeting constraint satisfaction (vs. 61% for PyRoki); these collision-free motions yield locomotion policies with 21% lower tracking error than PyRoki and 12x lower cross-seed variance than GMR. A ground-up codebase redesign for discoverability enabled LLM coding assistants to author up to 73% of new modules, including hand-optimized CUDA kernels, demonstrating that well-structured robotics code can unlock productive human-LLM collaboration. Together, these advances provide a unified, dynamics-aware motion generation stack that scales from single-arm manipulators to full humanoids. Code is available at https://github.com/NVlabs/curobo.
abstract_zh: 有效的机器人自主性需要安全、可行且反应式的运动生成。当前方法碎片化：快速规划器输出物理上不可执行的轨迹，反应式控制器难以处理高保真感知，现有求解器在高自由度系统上失败。我们提出cuRoboV2，一个统一框架，包含三项关键创新：(1) B样条轨迹优化，强制平滑性和力矩限制；(2) GPU原生TSDF/ESDF感知流水线，生成覆盖整个工作空间的密集有符号距离场，不同于仅提供稀疏分配块内距离的现有方法，在操作规模上比最先进技术快10倍且内存减少8倍，碰撞召回率高达99%；(3) 可扩展的GPU原生全身计算，即拓扑感知运动学、可微逆动力学和映射归约自碰撞，实现高达61倍加速，同时扩展到高自由度人形机器人（此前GPU实现失败）。在基准测试中，cuRoboV2在3kg负载下达到99.7%成功率（基线仅72-77%），48自由度人形机器人上99.6%无碰撞逆运动学（先前方法完全失败），89.5%重定向约束满足（PyRoki为61%）；这些无碰撞运动产生的行走策略跟踪误差比PyRoki低21%，跨种子方差比GMR低12倍。为可发现性而进行的底层代码库重构使LLM编码助手能够编写高达73%的新模块，包括手调优CUDA内核，表明结构良好的机器人代码可以解锁高效的人机LLM协作。这些进展提供了一个统一的、动力学感知的运动生成栈，从单臂机械臂扩展到全身人形机器人。代码见https://github.com/NVlabs/curobo。
tldr_zh: cuRoboV2统一了高自由度机器人的动力学感知运动生成，在负载、人形IK和重定向上大幅超越基线。
cover_zh: 高自由度机器人运动生成新突破｜cuRoboV2统一框架让负载成功率飙升至99.7%

judge.reason: 提出统一运动生成框架，支持高自由度人形机器人全身控制，GPU加速且开源，科研价值高。
